human_readable_summary: This ledger records what Mazur's 2015 blog post published (one widely-cited downstream anchor for post-update total error) and what backprop-trace's V8/Node 22 engine reproduces. The two differ by ~1.5e-7 at the 7th significant digit — the engine's value is what's emitted into receipts; the anchor is provided here for educational comparison. Drift on this claim is NOT a hard gate; bp verify mazur WARNs but does not FAIL when run against this published ledger. If your engine differs from Mazur by ~1.5e-7 on post_update_total_error, that's expected — see the drift_observed claim below.
engine_reproduction_source: fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl is the committed canonical first-run output of src/engine.ts (V8/Node 22). When this file documents drift against an anchor, the canonical engine value comes from the golden, not from this published ledger.

caveats_and_recommendations.activation: sigmoid (Math.exp via V8 fdlibm port; deterministic on Node 22 V8)
caveats_and_recommendations.optimizer: sgd (no momentum, no Adam, no weight decay)
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_engine_portability: V8 only; Hermes / JSC / Bun-JSC untested
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_node_major_portability: tested on Node 22.x only; Math.exp is not contractually bit-stable across V8 majors

standards_referenced: Pineau et al. JMLR 2021 — ML Reproducibility Checklist (https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12206)
Mitchell et al. FAT* 2019 — Model Cards (https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993)
Gebru et al. CACM 2021 — Datasheets for Datasets (https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010)
Kapoor et al. Science Advances 2024 — REFORMS (https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adk3452)
Jia et al. IEEE S&P 2021 — Proof-of-Learning (https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2103.05633)

source.author: Matt Mazur
source.title: A Step by Step Backpropagation Example

claim_type_legend.source_published_input: Constant input or hyperparameter explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_loss: Loss value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_forward: Forward-pass value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_update: Post-update parameter value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.widely_cited_downstream_anchor: Value commonly repeated in downstream pedagogy; not given an explicit derivation in the source at this precision

reproduction_status_legend.verified: Engine reproduces this value within tolerance
reproduction_status_legend.drift_observed: Engine produces a different value; documented in drift_note. Hard-gate value (true/false) governs whether drift fails the build or merely WARNs.

claims.provenance: Commonly repeated in downstream pedagogy and tutorials derived from Mazur's example. Mazur's blog does not give an explicit derivation of the post-update total error at this precision in step 1 — the value appears in many secondary sources but its computational lineage is not pinned in the primary source.
claims.drift_note: Engine first-run (src/engine.ts on V8/Node 22, IEEE-754 doubles, sigmoid via 1/(1+Math.exp(-x))) produces 0.29102777369359933 after step 1. The widely-cited anchor 0.291027924 differs by ~1.5e-7 absolute. Hypothesis (from prior research): 0.291027924 reflects a different forward-recomputation pathway or different intermediate rounding from Mazur's hand-derived chain. The engine's value is the canonical first-run output and lives in fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl; the anchor in this ledger is recorded for honesty rather than gating. v0.1 ships with hard_gate = false: drift triggers WARN in bp verify mazur (which lands next slice), never FAIL. v0.2 may resolve by sourcing 0.291027924's derivation lineage and reclassifying.
claims.v0_1_behavior: WARN on drift in bp verify mazur; build does not fail.
